title: salix × schumanniana
intro: Salix × schumanniana, also known as Schumann's willow, is a hybrid willow plant that is a cross between the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the white willow (Salix alba). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds that have been used to treat a range of health issues. The bark and leaves of Salix × schumanniana have been used in herbal preparations to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat conditions such as rheumatism and arthritis. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and tinctures, have been used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and improve overall health.
